mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher aggregate corporate earnings and supporting positive equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment is a core component of NBER business cycle dating.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) signals an imminent decline in consumer spending, which comprises the largest part of the economy. This leads to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately two-thirds of U.S. GDP.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns, indicating the market is looking through the weak data.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is actively raising the federal funds rate, higher borrowing costs and tighter financial conditions compress equity valuations via a higher discount rate, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in FEDFUNDS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a full tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in inflation (stagflation) crushes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, causing a sharp decline in equity markets.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY % change < 0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 4%
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5% from its 12-month low is a robust real-time indicator of a recession (Sahm Rule), triggering equity market sell-offs as investors price in a severe downturn.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of months where the trigger condition is met, suggesting false signals or priced-in recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) signal strong consumer balance sheets, confidence, and broad economic momentum due to the sector's large multiplier effect, which supports equity prices over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing construction has a large economic multiplier effect.
failure_condition: The 12-month change in HOUST shows a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is very tight (low UNRATE) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, it signals an overheating economy, forcing the Fed to tighten policy more aggressively than expected, which is negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed's dual mandate implies a policy reaction to this state.
failure_condition: The triggered signal does not predict negative returns with statistical significance (t-stat < -1.65) in a walk-forward test over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive year-over-year growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is the primary driver of aggregate corporate earnings growth, creating a fundamental tailwind for the S&P 500 over very short horizons as the market reacts to headline data.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity returns are a claim on future economic output.
failure_condition: The quarterly year-over-year change in GDPC1 has a rank IC below 0.03 with 5-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.
